title: Create 'RAT G700' build tool app (safe, non-malicious) with full-stack CRUD and export

requirements.text: Clarify and enforce that the application named "RAT G700" is a legitimate build/configuration tool and does not implement any remote access trojan (RAT), spyware, credential theft, persistence, covert control, or other malware behavior. In the UI, present a first-run configuration screen that asks the user to describe what "RAT G700" stands for and what a "build" means (e.g., parts list, steps, firmware config), and store these answers as app metadata used only for labeling and help text.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: On first load (no existing metadata), the app shows a setup screen asking for: app meaning/description, what a build represents, and preferred units/currency (optional).
The setup answers are persisted in the Motoko canister and reused to label the UI (e.g., page title/help text).
No features that enable covert device control, remote shells, keylogging, credential capture, or persistence are implemented.

requirements.text: Implement core data model and backend APIs for managing "Builds" and their "Items". A Build must support: id, name, description/notes, createdAt, updatedAt, and a list of Items. Each Item must support: id, label/name, quantity (number), and optional fields for cost and notes.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes canister methods to create/read/update/delete Builds.
Backend exposes methods to add/update/remove Items within a Build.
All timestamps are set/updated consistently on create/update operations.
Backend methods return Result-style responses (success/error) with user-friendly error strings for common failures (missing id, invalid quantity, etc.).

requirements.text: Create a frontend UI to manage Builds: list view (search/filter by name), create build, edit build, delete build (with confirmation), and a build detail view to manage its Items (add/edit/remove, quantities).

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: A Builds page shows all builds with a search box and a button to create a new build.
Selecting a build opens a detail view where Items can be added, edited inline or via modal, and removed with confirmation.
UI shows loading and error states for all backend interactions.
All user-facing text is in English.

requirements.text: Add export/import utilities for Builds: allow exporting a Build to a downloadable JSON file and importing a JSON file to create a new Build. Validate imported JSON and show validation errors without crashing.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: From a Build detail view, user can click Export and download a .json file containing the Build and its Items.
User can import a .json file; if valid, a new Build is created in the backend; if invalid, UI shows specific validation errors.
Import/export format is stable and includes a version field (e.g., schemaVersion).

requirements.text: Integrate Internet Identity sign-in and scope data per authenticated principal so each user only sees their own Builds.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users are prompted to sign in with Internet Identity to use the app.
After sign-in, Builds are stored and queried under the caller principal.
Signing out and signing in as another identity shows a different (isolated) dataset.

requirements.text: Apply a coherent, distinctive visual theme for the app (not blue/purple): choose a consistent palette, typography, and layout style appropriate for a build/configuration tool, and apply it across pages and components.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: Theme is consistently applied across navigation, forms, tables/lists, and dialogs.
Color palette avoids blue/purple as primary brand colors.
UI remains accessible (readable contrast, clear focus states).

requirements.text: Add a simple in-app Help/About page that explains what the app does based on the stored metadata from setup, and includes a clear statement that the app is not a remote-access tool and does not control devices.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: Help/About page displays the user-provided app description from setup.
Help/About includes an explicit non-malware statement.
Page is reachable from main navigation.

requirements.text: Provide a complete, runnable full-stack codebase: frontend and Motoko backend wired together with React Query for data fetching/mutations, and basic developer documentation on how to run locally and deploy on the Internet Computer.

quotes: rat g700 rat type builld tool all full code

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend uses React Query for all canister data operations (queries + mutations) with cache invalidation.
A README (or equivalent) exists with steps to run locally (dfx start/deploy) and access the frontend.
App builds and runs without placeholder broken links or missing routes.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui or the immutable hook/entry paths specified by the platform; compose them from outside instead.
Use only Internet Identity for authentication (no third-party auth).
Do not implement any remote access trojan functionality or any features that enable malware behavior.
nonGoals: Real-time collaboration or WebSocket-based live updates.
Any device control, remote shell, keylogging, credential capture, persistence mechanisms, or obfuscation techniques.

required: Minimalist monochrome logo combining a gear and a rat silhouette (rat-g700-logo.dim_512x512.png)
